# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.
# Category: container_security

# Start with an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3-slim  # you can use other base images if needed, e.g., alpine or debian based on your requirements.

# Set the working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app  

COPY . ./    # Copy current dir contents into container at /app folder in image registry (Faster Read/Write) 
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt     # Install python dependencies, replace with 'pip freeze > requirements.txt' if not using a virtual environment file for dependency management like venv or conda envs  
# Now it should work as long you run the Docker image and provide files inside /upload folder to upload  in container via docker command: `docker run -v $PWD/upload:/app/upload your_image`. You need replace 'your_img' with yours Image name, where '/path-to你的文件夹(/Uploads)' is the mount path of host machine and '. /YourDirectoryOnDockerContainer(requirements)'.
EXPOSE 80        # To make port accessible for users outside this container   
CMD [ "python" ]
